# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

const DEFAULT_PORT = 8000;

function jsonResponse(data: unknown, status = 200): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
      "cache-control": "no-store",
    },
  });
}

function textResponse(text: string, status = 200): Response {
  return new Response(text, {
    status,
    headers: {
      "content-type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
      "cache-control": "no-store",
    },
  });
}

function getConfiguredModules(url: URL): string[] {
  const envValue = Deno.env.get("PLUGIN_MODULES") ?? "";
  const queryValue = url.searchParams.get("modules") ?? "";

  const sources = [envValue, queryValue].filter(Boolean);
  const modules = sources
    .flatMap((source) => source.split(","))
    .map((s) => s.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);

  return [...new Set(modules)];
}

function isAllowedModuleSpec(spec: string): boolean {
  if (!spec) return false;
  if (spec.startsWith("data:")) return false;
  if (spec.startsWith("javascript:")) return false;
  if (spec.includes("\0")) return false;
  return true;
}

async function loadModule(spec: string): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
  if (!isAllowedModuleSpec(spec)) {
    throw new Error(`Disallowed module specifier: ${spec}`);
  }
  const mod = await import(spec);
  return mod as Record<string, unknown>;
}

async function invokePlugin(mod: Record<string, unknown>, input: unknown): Promise<unknown> {
  const candidates = [
    mod.default,
    mod.handle,
    mod.plugin,
    mod.run,
    mod.default?.handle,
    mod.default?.run,
  ];

  for (const candidate of candidates) {
    if (typeof candidate === "function") {
      return await candidate(input);
    }
  }

  return mod;
}

async function handleRequest(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);

  if (url.pathname === "/health") {
    return textResponse("ok");
  }

  const modules = getConfiguredModules(url);
  const input = {
    url: req.url,
    method: req.method,
    headers: Object.fromEntries(req.headers.entries()),
    query: Object.fromEntries(url.searchParams.entries()),
  };

  if (modules.length === 0) {
    return jsonResponse({
      ok: true,
      message: "No modules configured",
      available: ["/health", "/plugins"],
    });
  }

  const results: Array<{ module: string; ok: boolean; result?: unknown; error?: string }> = [];

  for (const spec of modules) {
    try {
      const mod = await loadModule(spec);
      const result = await invokePlugin(mod, input);
      results.push({ module: spec, ok: true, result });
    } catch (error) {
      results.push({
        module: spec,
        ok: false,
        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      });
    }
  }

  return jsonResponse({
    ok: true,
    modules: results,
  });
}

Deno.serve({ port: DEFAULT_PORT }, handleRequest);
